component_identification.content: Terraforming Mars base components include a Mars game board with global parameters (oxygen, temperature, oceans) and map spaces, player boards for production/resource tracking, corporation cards, project card deck, tiles (ocean, city, greenery, special), markers for players and global tracks, and resource cubes in denomination colors. Standard setup also includes milestone and award markers plus reference materials. Core resources tracked per player are megacredits, steel, titanium, plants, energy, and heat. Terraform Rating (TR) marker serves as both income baseline and major scoring track.

core_game_loop.content: Play proceeds in generations. In each generation, players draw project cards, decide which to buy into hand, then take turns performing 1 or 2 actions until all pass. Actions include playing project cards, using standard projects, converting heat, placing tiles, using blue-card actions, claiming milestones, and funding awards. After everyone passes, production phase grants resources according to tracks and TR. Then a new generation begins. Game end is triggered when oxygen, temperature, and ocean parameters are all fully completed.

detailed_rules.content: Project cards require paying megacredit cost and meeting any requirements (tags, global parameter thresholds, or board conditions). Steel and titanium can be spent on eligible building/space tags according to printed conversion rates. Raising global parameters generally increases TR by 1 each step, improving both score and future MC income. Standard projects are always available but usually less efficient than card-driven engines. Tile placement obeys map restrictions: oceans only on ocean spaces, cities not adjacent to other cities, greeneries with adjacency preference to own tiles when legal, and placement bonuses resolved immediately from board icons. Blue cards may grant actions usable once each generation or persistent effects; red events are one-time plays; green cards are active projects with static effects/tags. Milestones can be claimed by meeting conditions and paying cost, with limited slots and first-come timing. Awards are funded during game and scored at end by ranking among players. End-game trigger occurs when all three terraforming parameters are complete; finish the current generation so each player gets equal action count. Edge cases frequently involve requirement checks at play time only, and card text overriding default placement or production rules. Teaching clarifications from rulebook-first play: always resolve effects in printed timing windows and finish mandatory steps before optional effects. If a card or tile text conflicts with a baseline rule, the specific card text takes priority for that interaction only. During teaching, call out hidden information boundaries, legal action checks before payment, and tie handling exactly as written. Most play disputes come from skipped sequence steps, so keep turn order visible and confirm each phase aloud in early rounds.

scoring_and_endgame.content: Final score equals TR plus additional VP from played cards, board presence, milestones, and awards. Board points include 1 VP per greenery and city adjacency points for neighboring greeneries (regardless of owner as per rules). Award points are distributed by rank among qualified players; milestone points are fixed for claimed tiles. Highest total wins; tie-breaker is most megacredits. Because TR is both economy and scoring, early terraforming can snowball into stronger late-game VP conversion.

beginner_strategy.content: Early generations should prioritize production ramp (especially MC and key materials) over immediate vanity points. Be selective when buying cards during draft/draw; paying 3 MC per kept card is a hidden tax that can cripple tempo. Align card purchases with your corporation ability and opening tags rather than chasing every strong-looking card. Contest easy milestones early—waiting often loses guaranteed points. Use board placement tactically: cities and greeneries can create both your points and denial pressure. In endgame, convert all lingering resources into direct VP actions (greeneries, point cards, city placement) before trigger closure. New-player coaching tip: choose one clear plan by the end of early game, then spend midgame turns increasing action efficiency rather than collecting disconnected benefits. Track remaining rounds/end triggers constantly; many losses come from mis-timing rather than bad tactical choices. When uncertain between two similar options, prefer the move that preserves flexibility for the next turn while still scoring now.

metadata.notes: Rewrite sprint using official rulebook text as primary reference.
